seoTitle: How to Create a GoHighLevel Account
metaDescription: A step-by-step walkthrough for signing up for GoHighLevel, choosing a plan, and getting your account set up for the first time in 2026.
intro: Getting started with GoHighLevel begins with setting up your account correctly so you're ready to build funnels, manage contacts, and automate workflows. This guide covers the sign-up process from start to finish.

steps.heading: Go to the GoHighLevel sign-up page
steps.body: Start the process by navigating to GoHighLevel's sign-up flow, either directly or through an agency or affiliate link if you were referred.

steps.heading: Choose a plan
steps.body: Select the plan that matches your needs, since GoHighLevel offers different tiers depending on how many features and sub-accounts you require.

steps.heading: Enter your account details
steps.body: Provide your business information and contact details to create the account.

steps.heading: Set up billing
steps.body: Enter your payment information to activate your subscription, noting any trial period that may apply.

steps.heading: Complete the initial account setup
steps.body: Walk through GoHighLevel's onboarding steps to configure basic settings like your business profile before diving into the platform.

faq.question: Does GoHighLevel offer a free trial?
faq.answer: GoHighLevel has offered trial periods on some plans, so check the current sign-up page for the latest trial terms before entering payment details.

faq.question: What information do I need to sign up?
faq.answer: You'll typically need basic business details and a payment method to complete the sign-up and activate your account.

faq.question: Can I upgrade or downgrade my plan later?
faq.answer: Yes, GoHighLevel allows you to change plans after your account is created if your needs change.

faq.question: Is GoHighLevel meant for agencies or individual businesses?
faq.answer: It supports both, with plans and sub-account structures designed for agencies managing multiple clients as well as individual businesses running their own operations.
